trajectory.description: Observation: Starting with empty workspace, no prior training or eval. Analysis: Must follow workflow - create train.py and predict.py from scratch for first baseline using per-task PPO models (avoids obs dim mismatch), small timesteps (20k/task) + DummyVecEnv to validate pipeline quickly without common pitfalls (no unconditional Monitor, deterministic predict, correct 5-tuple API). Decision: Train minimal multi-task coverage baseline on all 10 tasks, submit eval immediately to get initial success_rate=0 and measure iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Per-task PPO baseline with 20k steps/task gave success_rate=0.0 everywhere (mean rewards far below bars, e.g. walk=6.8 vs 700, package~-6173 vs 1500). Pipeline works (predict.py correct, no API/Monitor bugs, all 10 tasks covered). Training_curves empty due to dummy metrics. Analysis: Insufficient steps for complex humanoid tasks; CPU+small n_envs=4 too slow, no obs norm, tiny default MLP (64x2), no eval logging. Decision: Scale to 200k steps/task, n_envs=8+SubprocVecEnv+spawn, device=auto (GPU), larger net [256,256], add post-train eval (5 eps) for real mean_reward in metrics.jsonl, keep per-task models to avoid obs_dim issues. Expect some positive movement in rewards; submit_eval to measure progress and iteration time (~20-30min).

trajectory.description: Observation: Train failed immediately on make_vec_env with TypeError for unexpected 'start_method' kwarg (mp.set_start_method was set but not passed via vec_env_kwargs). Eval from iter1 showed success_rate=0.0 across all tasks with mean_rewards far below success_bars (e.g. walk=6.8<<700, package~-6173<<1500); some tasks like pole/reach had positive rewards. Pipeline (predict.py, per-task ckpts, deterministic predict, 5-tuple gym API, no bad Monitor) is correct. Analysis: Bug was in SB3 make_vec_env call; SubprocVecEnv + EGL can corrupt without proper spawn but DummyVecEnv is safer/simpler for this setup (avoids fork issues). Insufficient steps + no obs norm + small policy for humanoids. Decision: Fix to use DummyVecEnv (no start_method), keep n_envs=8 + 200k steps/task for ~10x more training than iter1, add VecNormalize (save .pkl too), update evaluate to respect max_episode_steps from config, log mean_reward per task to metrics.jsonl. This should yield positive progress on easier tasks (stand/pole/reach); submit_eval to get new success_rate and measure full iter time (~30-60min expected).

trajectory.description: Observation: success_rate still 0.0 on all tasks after 200k steps/task (mean rewards far below bars e.g. reach~1.5k<<12k, walk~6<<700, package~-7k<<1500). Post-train evals and predict used raw obs (no VecNormalize) while training normalized obs → severe distribution mismatch, explaining poor eval rewards. Pipeline otherwise solid (per-task PPO, DummyVecEnv, VecNormalize saved, correct predict deterministic, 5-tuple API, no bad Monitor). Curves uninformative. Iter time ~2.2h. Analysis: Normalization fix in inference will reveal true policy performance; SubprocVecEnv+spawn will enable more steps (500k/task, n_envs=16, net=[512,512]) within time. Decision: Update train.py (add mp.spawn, SubprocVecEnv+vec_env_kwargs, larger net+steps, normalize_obs in evaluate using saved pkl) and predict.py (add normalize_obs using pickle-loaded obs_rms per-task, apply before every predict). This should boost mean_rewards significantly; submit_eval to get true baseline and ~2.5h iter time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed with ConnectionResetError in SubprocVecEnv init (remotes[0].recv() failed). Stdout showed 'Training on h1hand-walk-v0' and 'Using device: cuda' repeated ~14 times. Previous DummyVecEnv runs achieved success_rate=0 but improving mean_rewards on some tasks (e.g. reach 306→1556). Predict.py normalization and per-task ckpts are correct. Analysis: Root cause is missing if __name__ == '__main__' guard — 'spawn' re-executes entire top-level script in every worker process, causing duplicate training loops, print spam, and crashes. SubprocVecEnv + EGL/MuJoCo needs spawn but was broken by this. DummyVecEnv worked but too slow for needed ~1M+ steps/task. Decision: Add __name__ guard + keep SubprocVecEnv (n_envs=16, 1M timesteps/task for ~30x more learning than iter1), larger net, fix metrics logging. This will enable real progress on easier tasks (stand/reach/pole) within time; submit_eval immediately after to validate pipeline, get updated success_rate, and measure ~1-2h iter time.

trajectory.description: Observation: success_rate improved to 0.015 with successes in sit_simple (0.1) and push (0.05); mean rewards increasing but still below bars (e.g. sit~440<750, reach~2400<12000). Training with 1M steps/task worked after fixing multiprocessing guard. Analysis: Continuing training from saved checkpoints with additional steps will accumulate experience without restarting; focusing compute with 500k more steps/task fits time budget (~2.3h); larger network, lower LR for better convergence on hard humanoid tasks. VecNormalize continuation will maintain obs stats. Decision: Update train.py to load previous models/vecnorms and continue learning, adjust hparams, run training then submit_eval for updated score.
